# Scorched-Earth ErdŇĎs Sweep ‚ÄĒ Turn 119

This is turn 119. Turns 1 through 118 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL ‚ÄĒ use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history‚Ä¶md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 119.

## Turn 119 ‚ÄĒ the outer Fourier transform is supported only on the zero-edge subspace

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit; proof-level exponents mean the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega_2\), while \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue. 

Retain the permanent active-block notation

\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,\qquad V=M/T_0,
\]

and

\[
B=Y/R,\qquad C=Y/K,\qquad \bar B=B/T_0.
\]

Put

\[
D_R=(R^\vee)^B,\qquad D_0=(T_0^\vee)^C.
\]

For \(0\ne\lambda\in D_R\), let \(\epsilon_\lambda\in H^1(\bar B,T_0^\vee)\) be the filtration-one edge of the scalar pushout along \(\lambda\).

### 1. The radical-edge assignment is linear

Turn 118 proved that inflation induces an isomorphism

\[
H^1(C,T_0^\vee)\xrightarrow{\sim}H^1(\bar B,T_0^\vee),
\]

because \(\operatorname{Hom}_C(V,T_0^\vee)=0\). Hence write uniquely

\[
\epsilon_\lambda=\operatorname{inf}(\bar\epsilon_\lambda),
\qquad
\bar\epsilon_\lambda\in H^1(C,T_0^\vee).
\]

The assignment

\[
\mathcal E:D_R\longrightarrow H^1(C,T_0^\vee),
\qquad
\lambda\longmapsto\bar\epsilon_\lambda
\tag{119.1}
\]

is \(\mathbf F_2\)-linear. Indeed, pushing the \(R\)-extension out along \(\lambda\) is linear in \(\lambda\), and taking the filtration-one component is a linear operation on its cohomology class.

Define the **determinant-relevant outer subspace**

\[
D_{\det}:=\ker\mathcal E.
\tag{119.2}
\]

Thus the zero-edge versus nonzero-edge dichotomy is not map-dependent or source-dependent. It is a linear decomposition already present in the finite target.

### 2. One character-sum identity contains both branches

Fix an exact lower map \(\rho:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow C\). Let

\[
h_\rho:V_\rho\longrightarrow \operatorname{Ob}_\rho(T_0)
\]

be the accessibility obstruction. For each accessible \(V\)-lift \(c\in h_\rho^{-1}(0)\), let \(\mathcal T_c\) be its affine \(T_0\)-lift torsor, of cardinality \(z_{T_0}\).

For the scalar obstruction \(\operatorname{ob}_{\lambda,c}\) on \(\mathcal T_c\),

\[
\#\{t\in\mathcal T_c:\operatorname{ob}_{\lambda,c}(t)=0\}
=
\frac{z_{T_0}}2+
\frac12\sum_{t\in\mathcal T_c}
(-1)^{\operatorname{ob}_{\lambda,c}(t)}.
\tag{119.3}
\]

Turn 118‚Äôs variation formula and perfect common-word duality imply

\[
\sum_{t\in\mathcal T_c}
(-1)^{\operatorname{ob}_{\lambda,c}(t)}
=
\begin{cases}
0,&\mathcal E(\lambda)\ne0,\\[2mm]
z_{T_0}(-1)^{F_{\rho,\lambda}(c)},&\mathcal E(\lambda)=0.
\end{cases}
\tag{119.4}
\]

The first line is ordinary character orthogonality on the \(T_0\)-torsor. The second holds because a zero edge makes the scalar obstruction independent of the chosen \(T_0\)-lift. 

Since

\[
z_{T_0}\,|h_\rho^{-1}(0)|=z_M,
\]

summing (119.3) gives the unified pointwise formula

\[
\boxed{
N_{\Gamma,\lambda}(\rho)
=
\frac{z_M}{2}
+
\mathbf 1_{\lambda\in D_{\det}}\,
\frac{z_{T_0}|B_\rho^1(V)|\,G_\Gamma(Q_\lambda)}
     {2|D_0|}
\sum_{a\in D_0}
(-1)^{\rho^*\Delta_{\lambda,a}}
}
\tag{119.5}
\]

for every \(0\ne\lambda\in D_R\).

When \(\lambda\notin D_{\det}\), this is exactly Turn 118‚Äôs pointwise half-fibre. When \(\lambda\in D_{\det}\), it is precisely Turn 117‚Äôs inner-\(D_0\) Fourier formula. 

Summing over exact lower maps yields

\[
\boxed{
\begin{aligned}
Z_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B/C)
={}&\frac{z_M}{2}|X_\Gamma(C)|\\
&+\mathbf 1_{\lambda\in D_{\det}}\,
\frac{z_{T_0}|B^1(V)|\,G_\Gamma(Q_\lambda)}
     {2|D_0|}
\sum_{a\in D_0}
\bigl(2n_{\Gamma,\lambda,a}-|X_\Gamma(C)|\bigr).
\end{aligned}}
\tag{119.6}
\]

Thus Proposition 4 no longer needs two independently narrated branches: the determinant correction is simply supported on \(\ker\mathcal E\).

### 3. The outer Fourier transform collapses

The Radical‚ÄďFrattini lemma gives

\[
T_0\le \Phi(B).
\]

Hence exact surjectivity to \(B\) is constant on each complete \(T_0\)-lift torsor. Therefore, for every \(\lambda\notin D_{\det}\),

\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)=\frac{|X_\Gamma(B)|}{2},
\]

and its signed outer Fourier coefficient vanishes:

\[
2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)-|X_\Gamma(B)|=0.
\tag{119.7}
\]

Consequently the outer inversion may be restricted from all of \(D_R\) to the linear subspace \(D_{\det}\):

\[
\boxed{
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma(B):o_R(f)=0\}
=
\frac1{|D_R|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D_{\det}}
\left(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)-|X_\Gamma(B)|\right),
}
\tag{119.8}
\]

with \(m_{\Gamma,0}(B)=|X_\Gamma(B)|\).

This is the structural pattern behind the last four turns:

- outer characters detected by the scalar radical cancel identically by torsor orthogonality;
- only characters annihilating that edge reach the determinant/simple-head machinery;
- only those characters require the inner \(D_0\)-Fourier transform and the smaller \(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\)-covers.

So the expensive part of the proof is intrinsically confined to \(D_{\det}\), potentially a much smaller subspace than \(D_R\). No finite-group patch or modification of the relation is indicated.

**ESTABLISHED**: Defined the linear radical-edge map \(\mathcal E:D_R\to H^1(C,T_0^\vee)\) and proved that the entire outer Fourier transform is supported on \(D_{\det}=\ker\mathcal E\). Derived the unified pointwise formula (119.5), containing both Turn 117‚Äôs zero-edge double-Fourier term and Turn 118‚Äôs nonzero-edge half-fibre. Nonzero-edge outer characters have identically zero signed Fourier coefficient and require no determinant or auxiliary-cover calculation.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the amended manuscript has not received an independent line-by-line review.

**NEXT**: Replace Proposition 4‚Äôs casewise prose by (119.1)‚Äď(119.8), restrict the outer Fourier stage explicitly to \(D_{\det}\), and freeze the six-proposition theorem before submitting the unchanged candidate block.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
